name: disjoint_mappings_external_body_unsound
code:
proof fn phi_1_disjoint_mappings_external_body_unsound(d: Directory, i: nat, j: nat, va: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < j,
        j < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
        d.entries[j as int] is Page,
        d.entry_base(i) == d.entry_base(j),
    ensures
        !d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(d.entry_base(i)) || !d.interp_of_entry(j).contains_key(d.entry_base(j)),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_disjoint_mappings(i, j);
}
reason: `lemma_interp_of_entry_disjoint_mappings` is `external_body` and claims disjoint keys for all entry pairs — but the invariant has no alignment check on `base_vaddr` (commented out), so two Page entries could compute the same `entry_base` if `entry_size == 0` were possible or `base_vaddr` is pathological

name: interp_of_entry_between_no_alignment_guard
reason: `lemma_interp_of_entry_between` is `external_body` and claims `va + pte.frame.size <= next_entry_base(i)` — but `pages_match_entry_size` only constrains `frame.size`, and for Directory entries the subdirectory's mappings have no alignment or containment proof backing this bound

name: inv_allows_unaligned_base_vaddr
reason: `well_formed` has the alignment check on `base_vaddr` commented out — a directory with `base_vaddr = 1` satisfies `inv()`, undermining the external_body lemmas that implicitly assume alignment

name: page_frame_base_unconstrained
reason: The invariant checks `frame.size == entry_size` but never constrains `frame.base` — PTEs can map to unaligned physical addresses since `frames_aligned` is commented out in `inv()`

name: entry_containment_allows_cross_entry_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_containment_allows_cross_entry_mapping(d: Directory, i: nat, va: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_pair(va, pte),
    ensures
        d.entry_base(i) <= va,
        d.entry_base(i) < va + pte.frame.size,
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_between(i, va, pte);
}
reason: `lemma_interp_of_entry_between` claims `entry_base(i) < va + frame.size` (strict inequality) — but for a Page entry, `va == entry_base(i)` and `frame.size == entry_size`, giving `entry_base(i) < entry_base(i) + entry_size` which requires `entry_size > 0`; the external_body trusts this without deriving it from the invariant's `entry_size > 0` constraint
